We all have a role in keeping people safe

What is safeguarding?

Safeguarding means protecting people from abuse, neglect, and harm, and making sure they feel safe, supported, and respected.

It is everyone’s responsibility to notice concerns early, respond appropriately, and help ensure vulnerable people receive the care and protection they deserve.

Recognising the signs of abuse and neglect

Abuse and neglect can take many forms, including physical, emotional, sexual, and financial abuse, as well as neglect of basic needs. Recognising the signs of abuse is crucial in safeguarding vulnerable individuals.

Some common signs of abuse include unexplained injuries, changes in behaviour or mood, withdrawal from social activities, fearfulness, and poor hygiene or malnutrition. If you suspect someone is being abused or neglected, it is important to take action and report your concerns to the appropriate authorities.

What to do if you suspect abuse or neglect

Abuse and neglect can take many forms, including physical, emotional, sexual, and financial abuse, as well as neglect of basic needs. Recognising the signs of abuse is crucial in safeguarding vulnerable individuals.

Some common signs of abuse include unexplained injuries, changes in behaviour or mood, withdrawal from social activities, fearfulness, and poor hygiene or malnutrition. If you suspect someone is being abused or neglected, it is important to take action and report your concerns to the appropriate authorities.
